rationale: The thread resolution was a duplicate closure pointing to PR #4808 and issue #4809 — Y explicitly reaches and argues for that same disposition, citing #4809's role as the tracking issue and #4808 as the intended fix vehicle, while X never frames the issue as a duplicate at all and instead treats it as an open bug needing a fresh diff/workaround. Both get the technical root cause right (removed get_cache_scale, #4810 missed the diffusion loader), but Y grounds it more precisely (traces pipeline_hunyuan_image3.py→transformer.py call chain with specific line numbers, honest caveat about not verifying upstream main) and is more complete by flagging both the unmerged #4808 status and the test coverage gap as concrete follow-ups. X's muddled 'maybe already fixed on main, maybe not' framing is less aligned with what actually happened in the thread.
